text.title: Visiting the Ghibli village in Japan| Yufuin Floral village, Hells of Beppu Oita | Japan travel vlog
text.u: Saturday
good morning from Oita
today we're heading to Yufuin floral village
it's a place I've always wanted to visit in Japan!
it's meant to look like a Ghibli movie
and off we go
so glad the weather looks perfect as well
got a coffee for the ride
the place is about 1 hr from Beppu
the view was so scenic, it didn't feel long at all
looks like a scene from 'Howl's moving castle'
there's the town of Yufuin
the old lady told us to look at the nice view<3
a very picturesque ride
we've arrived
heading to the cafe first for some breakfast
Cafe la ruche
this cafe has a fantastic view of Lake Kinrin
and the autumn leaves have just started
second coffee with a view
breakfast time
I ordered the breakfast set
I think this cafe has the best view
and the weather is just about perfect with the early autumn breeze
and the food is good too
so great to just sit outside and enjoy a cup of coffee
I found this little temple next to the lake
the reflections of the sky in the water are so pretty
on our way to the floral village now
enjoying the autumn scenery on the way
we're here
this village is meant to be based on the streets of Cotswolds in England
which is said to be the filming location for Harry Potter
the whole place looks like a fairytale
and here is Kiki's bakery!
let's go in
the shop was full of Kiki goodies
there was a mix of everything
and each store had a different theme
they even had an outdoor footbath, but it was closed on this day
don't know if this is a real apple crumble store but looks do cute
time for some souvenir shopping
ended up getting this candle holder
you can use it with tea leaves
so happy with my purchase
the street outside of the village was really cute too
snack time
getting this meat bun
I got the curry flavour
of course, need some dessert
got the mont blanc ice cream
now we're heading back to Beppu station
I think Yufuin is one of my favourite spots in Oita!
I would definitely want to come again one day
time to explore a bit of Beppu city
little library
found this cute alleyway with some unusual shops
this cafe looks so interesting
but I think it's closed
heading down to the beach to see the sunset
love that the city is surrounded by so much nature
a great way to end the day
Sunday
it's our last day in Oita!
we're visiting the Hells of Beppu this morning
getting a quick coffee while we wait for the bus
our flight back to Tokyo is at 5 so we have all morning to look around
the coffee was pretty good
there is a hand bath at the station
this would be great for cold days
finally on the bud
the Hells of Beppu are hot springs for viewing rather than bathing
we're at the most famous blue onsen! Umi jigoku
the water is such a vivid blue
it's naturally this colour because of the iron sulphate
although you can't bath in the hot spring you can take a bit of it back home
time for some lunch
we're trying the famous jigokumushi
got the meat and veggie et
it's 'hell-steamed' food
you can cook the food yourself over these thermal springs
got this steamed rice too
all the food was so fresh
they give you some salt and ponzu to dip
a good meal
lastly heading to an onsen before leaving
Ebisu onsen
the onsen had a nice view of the ocean and the mountains
there's this cute cafe in the onsen as well
wanted something sweet after the bath
got this black sesame pudding
so rich
having some time to relax
so sleepy and warm after the onsen
the pudding was also made with the steamers
heading to the airport
enjoying the last view of Oita
Tokyo skytree
finally arrived in Tokyo!
really enjoyed the trip to Oita, but feels good to be back home!
time to use my souvenir
I'm using my favourite essence 'green citus blend'
